recentHighlights: Business as Usual (S5E18) — Quark's brief and uncomfortable career in weapons sales. Deeply in debt, he accepts a job from his cousin Gaila — a weapons dealer — and discovers he's a natural salesman. But the Regent of Palamar wants twenty-eight million people dead, and Quark's conscience catches up. His guilt-dream — walking into his bar to find everyone dead, O'Brien carrying his dead baby — is the guilt apparatus made literal. Quark escapes not by confronting Hagath but by making the system no longer want him. Written by Thompson & Weddle, directed by Alexander Siddig.

Ties of Blood and Water (S5E19) — Tekeny Ghemor returns to DS9 to die of Yarim Fel syndrome. The episode intercuts his death with flashbacks to Kira's biological father Taban's death during the Occupation — two fathers, two deaths. Kira nearly fails him again when Dukat reveals Ghemor's war record (Kiessa Monastery massacre), but Odo and Bashir bring her back. Kira buries Ghemor next to Taban, the once-withered tree now green. "No one deserves to die alone." Written by Robert Hewitt Wolfe, directed by Avery Brooks.

Ferengi Love Songs (S5E20) — Ira Steven Behr & Hans Beimler's Ferengi family farce with real emotional weight. The Nagus's cognitive decline as state secret: Ishka runs the Ferengi economy from a bedroom while Zek feigns competence with her prepared answers. Quark's sabotage of his mother's relationship for profit, the 199-point market freefall. Rom's choice to renounce his fortune for Leeta — love over latinum. The closet as the archive of Ferengi secrets. Directed by Rene Auberjondois.

Doctor Bashir, I Presume? (S5E16) — Bashir's genetic engineering reveal: his parents had him illegally resequenced at age seven, his entire brilliance manufactured. Zimmerman arrives to model the LMH after Bashir; the secret emerges through a hologram. Richard takes a two-year sentence to protect Julian's career. Rom tells Leeta he loves her. O'Brien's friendship survives the lie: "From now on... you play from back here. I'll play from up there." Written by Ronald D. Moore, directed by Livingston.

A Simple Investigation (S5E17) — Rene Echevarria's Odo noir. An Orion Syndicate operative named Arissa comes to the station; Odo falls into genuine intimacy — his first sexual experience with a humanoid. The bittersweet reveal: her memories were wiped by Idanian Intelligence; the woman he loved was a constructed identity. She recovers her memories, reveals she has a husband, and leaves. The question: is the person you loved who she was during the suppression the "real" her, or did you love a fabrication?

By Inferno's Light (S5E15) — The Dominion War's second punch: Cardassia joins the Dominion, Dukat disowns Ziyal, Garak conquers his claustrophobia in a crawlspace to finish Tain's work. The Changeling Bashir nearly destroys the Bajoran sun. The false-invasion trap — warp signatures that don't correspond to real ships. Martok re-born, alliances rebuilt. Written by Behr & Wolfe, directed by Landau.

For the Uniform (S5E13) — Sisko threatens to poison a Maquis world — and does it. The bluff is indistinguishable from the real thing. Eddington surrenders not because he's trapped but because the script demands it: Valjean must sacrifice himself for Javert. Written by Peter Allan Fields, directed by Lobl.
